subject: Mathematics
title: Is the Universe Infinite?
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Is the Universe Infinite?.

hook.text: Does space go on forever, or does it wrap around like the surface of a sphere?

buildup.text: Current measurements show the universe is flat to within 0.4%—suggesting it might be infinite.

discovery.text: But flat doesn't guarantee infinite. A flat torus is finite yet has no edges.

twist.text: We can only see 93 billion light-years across. What lies beyond the observable universe is unknown.

climax.text: Whether the universe is finite or infinite may be permanently unknowable from inside it.

punchline.text: We live in a universe whose size we may never measure.

quiz.question: Why can't we determine if the universe is truly infinite?
options: We can only observe a limited region of it
